commands.review_summary:
The branch adds `ORACLE_CHATGPT_ACCOUNT_EMAIL` handling to Welcome back login so the browser picker selects a matching saved ChatGPT account, with unit tests for matching and missing-account cases.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: with account chips like `steipete@gmail.com` and `pete@gmail.com`, preferred `pete@gmail.com` can match the first chip because the PR uses `includes`. No live browser proof was provided.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the narrow code defect is clear, but the external PR also needs contributor-supplied real browser proof and maintainer confirmation for the new env surface.

target.title: [codex] Prefer configured ChatGPT welcome-back account
target.body:
## Summary

- Thread the configured ChatGPT account email into the welcome-back account picker.
- Click the matching account chip instead of blindly selecting the first available account.
- Avoid falling back to the wrong account when the configured account is missing.

## Why

When several ChatGPT accounts are available on the welcome-back screen, the browser flow could restore the wrong session by clicking the first account chip. That defeats account-specific browser runs and can send prompts under the wrong ChatGPT account.

## Validation

- `pnpm test tests/browser/pageActions.test.ts`
- `pnpm run format:check`
- `pnpm run typecheck`
target.author: derekszen
